accounts.name: caller
docs: Only required to be the organizer when flipping status to Cancelled.
Once status == Cancelled, any signer can call to process refund chunks.

accounts.name: organizer_token_account
docs: Organizer's ATA in the tournament's token mint. Required only when an
unrefunded `organizer_deposit > 0` is being processed in this call.
Constraints (mint + owner) are validated in-handler so that callers
processing later refund chunks may pass `None`.

instructions.name: claim_result
docs: Permissionless: finalize an undisputed proposal after its dispute
window. Supply `placements` + payout ATAs when it is the final match.

instructions.name: confirm_result
docs: The counterparty accepts the proposal, finalizing the match. Supply
`placements` + payout ATAs (remaining_accounts) when it is the final.

accounts.name: next_match
docs: Required for non-final matches; pass `None` when finalizing the final.

accounts.name: token_mint
docs: SPL Token mint for the tournament's prize pool. Any valid SPL mint is
accepted (USDC, wSOL for native-SOL tournaments via wrap, custom).
Frontend gatekeeps user-facing token selection — on-chain trusts the
caller. `Account<Mint>` validates the account is a real Mint.

accounts.name: tournament
docs: Tournament PDA. `name` is used directly as a seed; capped at
`MAX_TOURNAMENT_NAME_LEN` (32) bytes — Solana's per-seed limit. Length
validated in the handler before account init.

accounts.name: organizer_token_account
docs: Optional organizer ATA used to fund `organizer_deposit`. Required when
`organizer_deposit > 0`; pass `None` to skip. Mint + owner constraints
guarantee the deposit is debited from the organizer's own funds in the
configured tournament token. Anchor 0.32 auto-unwraps `Option<Account>`
inside constraint expressions and skips the check when the account is
`None`, so we reference fields directly without explicit Option handling.

instructions.name: dispute_result
docs: The counterparty rejects the proposal, escalating to the organizer.

instructions.name: force_claim_disputed
docs: Permissionless backstop: finalize a disputed match the organizer never
resolved, 24h after the dispute.

accounts.name: treasury
docs: The actual ATA `(treasury, tournament.token_mint)` is derived at
distribution time per tournament.

accounts.name: default_mint
docs: Recommended default mint (e.g. USDC). Stored on `ProtocolConfig` as
advisory metadata — per-tournament `token_mint` is not constrained
against this.

accounts.name: game_identity_attestation
docs: credential + schema match `protocol_config`, nonce == player, not
expired). Required when `tournament.game != Manual`; pass `None` for
Manual tournaments.

instructions.name: migrate_v1_tournament
docs: Devnet upgrade-only: grow a pre-V1 Tournament account to the V1 layout.
Registration-phase tournaments only (see instruction docs).

accounts.name: tournament
docs: is shorter than the current `INIT_SPACE` and would fail Anchor's
deserialization. Validated below by program ownership + discriminator.

instructions.name: propose_result
docs: A match player records the result they claim, opening the dispute
window. Non-`OrganizerOnly` tournaments only.

accounts.name: next_match
docs: Required for non-final matches; pass `None` when reporting the final.

accounts.name: organizer_token_account
docs: Organizer's ATA in the tournament's token mint. Required on final-match
when `tournament.organizer_deposit > 0` and the deposit has not been
refunded yet (Variant A — deposit is excluded from the prize-pool
basis). Pass `None` for non-final reports or when the deposit is `0`.
Mint + owner are validated by Anchor (constraints auto-skip when None).

instructions.name: request_seed
docs: Bind a committed Switchboard randomness account to the tournament.
Opt-in; once bound, `start_tournament` requires the seed to be revealed.

accounts.name: randomness_account
docs: program and parse as a `RandomnessAccountData`.

instructions.name: resolve_dispute
docs: The organizer (arbitrator) settles a disputed match.

instructions.name: reveal_seed
docs: Permissionless: consume the revealed randomness as the bracket seed.
Must be bundled with Switchboard's reveal in the same transaction.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Permissionless fee-payer — anyone may push the reveal through.

accounts.name: randomness_account
docs: `RandomnessAccountData` below.

accounts.name: slot_hashes
docs: Read manually because deserializing the full Vec is expensive.

errors.name: UnauthorizedAuthority
errors.msg: Caller is not the authorized authority for this action

errors.name: TournamentFull
errors.msg: Tournament has reached its maximum participant count

errors.name: AlreadyRegistered
errors.msg: Wallet is already registered for this tournament

errors.name: RegistrationClosed
errors.msg: Registration window for this tournament is closed

errors.name: NotInRegistration
errors.msg: Tournament is not in the Registration state

errors.name: NotActive
errors.msg: Tournament is not in the Active state

errors.name: NotCompleted
errors.msg: Tournament is not in the Completed state

errors.name: InvalidPayoutPreset
errors.msg: Selected payout preset is invalid

errors.name: PresetExceedsParticipants
errors.msg: Selected payout preset requires more participants than configured

errors.name: MatchAlreadyReported
errors.msg: Match has already been reported

errors.name: NonParticipantWinner
errors.msg: Reported winner is not a participant of the tournament

errors.name: TournamentInProgress
errors.msg: Cannot cancel a tournament that has matches in progress

errors.name: RefundAlreadyIssued
errors.msg: Refund has already been issued to this participant

errors.name: MaxParticipantsExceeded
errors.msg: Participant count exceeds the protocol maximum (128)

errors.name: MinParticipantsNotMet
errors.msg: Participant count is below the protocol minimum (2)

errors.name: NameTooLong
errors.msg: Tournament name exceeds 32 bytes

errors.name: InvalidTokenMint
errors.msg: Provided token mint is invalid for this tournament

errors.name: InvalidVault
errors.msg: Provided vault token account does not match the tournament vault

errors.name: InvalidTreasury
errors.msg: Provided treasury token account does not match the protocol treasury

errors.name: InvalidMatchIndex
errors.msg: Match referenced is outside the bracket

errors.name: ParentMatchesNotComplete
errors.msg: Match parents not yet completed; cannot report this match

errors.name: RemainingAccountsMismatch
errors.msg: remaining_accounts does not match expected count for this instruction

errors.name: ArithmeticOverflow
errors.msg: Arithmetic overflow

errors.name: SlotHashesUnavailable
errors.msg: slot_hashes sysvar is empty; cannot derive seed

errors.name: GameNotYetSupported
errors.msg: Selected game is not yet supported for tournament creation

errors.name: AttestationRequired
errors.msg: This game requires a SAS identity attestation to join

errors.name: InvalidAttestationOwner
errors.msg: Attestation account is not owned by the SAS program

errors.name: WrongAttestationCredential
errors.msg: Attestation credential does not match the protocol's SAS credential

errors.name: WrongAttestationSchema
errors.msg: Attestation schema does not match the game's SAS schema

errors.name: AttestationWalletMismatch
errors.msg: Attestation nonce does not bind to the joining wallet

errors.name: AttestationExpired
errors.msg: Attestation has expired

errors.name: MalformedAttestation
errors.msg: Attestation account data is malformed

errors.name: SettlementModeMismatch
errors.msg: This action is not allowed for the tournament's settlement mode

errors.name: NotPlayerInMatch
errors.msg: Signer is not a player in this match

errors.name: NotCounterparty
errors.msg: Only the counterparty may confirm or dispute this proposal

errors.name: NoProposal
errors.msg: Match has no pending proposal

errors.name: ProposalAlreadyExists
errors.msg: Match already has a pending proposal

errors.name: InvalidProposedWinner
errors.msg: Proposed winner is not a player in this match

errors.name: ClaimWindowNotElapsed
errors.msg: Claim window has not elapsed yet

errors.name: ProposalDisputed
errors.msg: Proposal is disputed; it cannot be claimed

errors.name: ProposalNotDisputed
errors.msg: Proposal is not disputed

errors.name: SeedNotRevealed
errors.msg: Tournament seed has not been revealed; start is gated on VRF

errors.name: RandomnessNotResolved
errors.msg: Switchboard randomness is not yet resolved for this slot

errors.name: RandomnessAccountMismatch
errors.msg: Provided randomness account does not match the tournament commitment

errors.name: InvalidRandomnessOwner
errors.msg: Randomness account is not owned by the Switchboard On-Demand program

errors.name: MalformedRandomness
errors.msg: Randomness account data is malformed

errors.name: SeedAlreadyRevealed
errors.msg: Tournament seed has already been revealed

errors.name: InvalidTournamentAccount
errors.msg: Account is not a Tournament owned by this program

errors.name: MigrationNotNeeded
errors.msg: Tournament account is already at the V1 layout; migration not needed

fields.name: bracket
docs: Bracket lane — always `0` for single-elimination (V1). Part of the PDA
seed (C9). See `MatchNode::bracket`.

fields.name: bracket
docs: Bracket lane this node lives in. `0` for single-elimination (V1). Part
of the PDA seed (C9 schema-prep): future formats add a losers' bracket
(`1`) or per-group lanes without a second redeploy. Always `0` today.

fields.name: proposal_source
docs: Origin of the pending proposal. `None` ⇒ envelope empty.

fields.name: proposer
docs: Wallet that authored the pending proposal (a match player, or the
oracle's reporter key). `Pubkey::default()` when empty.

fields.name: proposed_winner
docs: Winner asserted by the pending proposal.

fields.name: proposed_at
docs: Unix time the proposal was recorded.

fields.name: claim_deadline
docs: Unix time after which a permissionless finalize is allowed. Set to
`proposed_at + dispute_window_secs` on propose; **re-armed** to
`now + FORCE_CLAIM_WINDOW_SECS` (24h) on dispute. The `disputed` flag
selects which permissionless ix may act past it: `claim_result` while
`!disputed`, `force_claim_disputed` while `disputed` (organizer silence
backstop). `resolve_dispute` ignores it — the organizer may act anytime.

fields.name: disputed
docs: Set by `dispute_result`; blocks `claim_result` and routes the match to
the organizer arbitrator (`resolve_dispute`), with a 24h
`force_claim_disputed` backstop against organizer silence.

fields.name: dispute_reason
docs: Free-form reason code supplied by the disputer (`0` = unspecified).
Surfaced by the indexer's notification kernel; not interpreted on-chain.

fields.name: bracket
docs: Bracket lane (C9). `0` for single-elimination (V1). The canonical
"advance the bracket" signal — emitted by every finalize path.

fields.name: identity_hash
docs: keccak hash of the SAS attestation's `identity_bytes`. Zero-bytes for
`Manual`-game tournaments (no attestation required).

fields.name: identity_attestation
docs: The SAS Attestation account that bound this wallet ↔ game identity.
`Pubkey::default()` for Manual tournaments.

fields.name: wins
docs: Foundation stats consumed by partial-cancel (make-whole survivors),
formats standings, and the webapp profile. Set by result-reporting ix.

types.name: ProposalSource
docs: Who authored the result currently sitting in a `MatchNode`'s proposal
envelope. The settlement flow treats every source uniformly — a proposal is
a proposal regardless of origin (C2: the Oracle is "just another proposer",
no parallel dispute system). Explicit discriminants are part of the wire
contract: the indexer and SDK decode by number.

fields.name: default_mint
docs: Recommended default token mint (advisory only — clients may show this
as the "default" / "preferred" mint in their UI). Per-tournament
`tournament.token_mint` is NOT constrained against this — any SPL
mint (USDC, wSOL, custom) can be used per tournament.

fields.name: sas_credential
docs: BracketChain's SAS Credential PDA (issuer = indexer's sas-issuer key).
`join_tournament` validates an attestation's credential against this.

fields.name: sas_schemas
docs: One SAS Schema PDA per `SupportedGame` variant, indexed by discriminant
(`sas_schemas[game as usize]`). `Manual` (index 0) is unused. Set via
`set_sas_config`; unset slots are `Pubkey::default()`.

fields.name: forced
docs: `true` when finalized via `force_claim_disputed` (post-dispute backstop)
rather than the ordinary undisputed `claim_result`.

fields.name: force_claim_deadline
docs: Re-armed deadline after which `force_claim_disputed` may finalize.

fields.name: source
docs: `ProposalSource` discriminant (1 = Player, 2 = Oracle, …).

fields.name: claim_deadline
docs: Deadline after which `claim_result` may permissionlessly finalize.

types.name: SettlementMode
docs: Who may report match results for a tournament. Locked at create-time.
`OrganizerOnly` (0) is the MVP behaviour and the only mode with a live
reporting path today; `PlayerReported` + `Oracle` ship their reporting
instructions in later V1 stages of this same redeploy. The enum ships now so
`tournament.settlement_mode` is positionally stable across the redeploy.

types.name: SupportedGame
docs: Game a tournament is played in. **Discriminants are a stable wire contract** —
indexers and the SAS game-identity schema lookup (`protocol_config.sas_schemas`)
index on these values, so never reorder or renumber. `Manual` (0) needs no
identity attestation; every other variant requires a SAS attestation at
`join_tournament`. Phase 1 only accepts `Manual` + `Dota2` at create-time
(the rest are reserved so the field stays positionally fixed).

fields.name: token_mint
docs: SPL Token mint for the prize pool. Any mint allowed (USDC, wSOL for
SOL tournaments, custom). Frontend gatekeeps user-facing selection.

fields.name: organizer_deposit
docs: Optional organizer top-up transferred into the vault at creation.
`0` is allowed. Treated as a refundable commitment (Variant A):
returned to the organizer on `cancel_tournament` (pre-start) AND on
`report_result` final-match. The deposit is excluded from the
prize-pool basis — protocol fee + placement payouts apply to
`vault.amount - organizer_deposit` only.

fields.name: organizer_deposit_refunded
docs: Set true once the deposit has been refunded — by `cancel_tournament`
(any-call, idempotent across chunks) or by `report_result` final-match.
Independent of per-participant `refund_paid` flags.

fields.name: game
docs: Game played; gates SAS identity requirement at `join_tournament`.

fields.name: settlement_mode
docs: Who may report results. Locked at create-time.

fields.name: dispute_window_secs
docs: Dispute window (seconds) for PlayerReported / Oracle settlement. Unused
by OrganizerOnly. Wired by the V1 player-reported stage of this redeploy.

fields.name: vrf_randomness_account
docs: Switchboard randomness account committed via `request_seed` (VRF stage).

fields.name: vrf_commit_slot
docs: Slot the VRF commitment was made at; `reveal_seed` reads after it passes.

fields.name: seed_revealed
docs: True once `reveal_seed` has populated `seed_hash` from VRF. Gates
`start_tournament` for non-OrganizerOnly tournaments.

fields.name: placement_payouts
docs: Per-placement breakdown: place=1..=N for prize tiers (champion is place=1).
Includes only non-zero payouts in CPI-execution order.

fields.name: treasury_recipient
docs: Treasury wallet receiving the protocol fee.
Self-contained event — indexers don't need extra reads.

fields.name: event_version
docs: Event wire version (C10). Always first field. See `EVENT_VERSION_V1`.

fields.name: name
docs: Human-readable tournament name (≤ MAX_TOURNAMENT_NAME_LEN bytes).
Indexers consume this to populate listing UIs without a follow-up RPC.

constants.name: EVENT_VERSION_V1
docs: Wire version stamped as the first field of every `#[event]` struct (C10).
The indexer parser rejects events whose `event_version` differs, preventing
silent Borsh mis-decode when the event layout changes in a future redeploy.

constants.name: FORCE_CLAIM_WINDOW_SECS
docs: Grace period after a dispute before `force_claim_disputed` lets anyone
finalize the proposed winner — the trustless backstop against an organizer
who never calls `resolve_dispute`. 24 hours.
